growth_records.cite: Nomura et al. Electron flow in hydrogenotrophic methanogens under nickel limitation. Nature (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41586-025-09229-y. [PMC12350162]
growth_records.snippet: For cultivation of M. thermolithotrophicus the standard medium 141 (H2/CO2) from DSMZ with modifications was used. This medium contains 0.34 g l-1 KCl, 4.00 g l-1 MgCl2.6H2O, 3.45 g l-1 MgSO4.7H2O, 0.25 g l-1 NH4Cl ...
growth_records.notes: Distantly related hydrogenotrophic methanogen used to test generality of the Ni-limitation electron-flow switch. Full defined medium (modified DSMZ 141) given verbatim. No numeric growth rate, temperature, pH, or flux stated in the extractable text; sodium acetate (1 g/L) present as carbon/reducing supplement typical of marine methanogen media. growth_rate_per_h null (no numeric mu). culture_mode set to batch as no continuous/chemostat operation is described for this organism (unlike M. marburgensis).

sec.method: chemostat, cell-specific CH4 production Vmax pooled across dilution rates/temperatures; headspace+dissolved CH4 by GC-FID

yields.note: no biomass yield reported; cell-specific rates only

growth_records.cite: Lucy C. Stewart et al. Fluid geochemistry, local hydrology, and metabolic activity define methanogen community size and composition in deep-sea hydrothermal vents. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0382-3. [PMC6776001]
growth_records.snippet: For M. thermolithotrophicus, the Vmax was 24 +/- 3 fmol CH4 cell-1 h-1 and the KS for H2 was 27 +/- 12 uM. ... the Ea was 73.8 kJ mol-1 and A was 3.36 x 10^11 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Experimental chemostat Monod: Vmax(CH4)=24+/-3 fmol/cell/h, KS(H2)=27+/-12 uM (pooled across 65 and 55 C). Uptake derived from 4H2+CO2->CH4+2H2O: qH2=4*24=96 fmol/cell/h, qCO2=24 fmol/cell/h (derived). Growth-rate CALCULATED from batch Arrhenius fit mu=A*exp(-Ea/RT), A=3.36e11 /h, Ea=73800 J/mol, T=338.15 K (65 C): Ea/RT=73800/(8.314*338.15)=26.25; mu=3.36e11*exp(-26.25)=3.36e11*3.97e-12=1.33 /h; td=ln2/1.33=0.52 h. Batch growth measured over 68-30 C. Microcosm data (cited ref 8) show these methanogens do not use formate/acetate; growth H2-limited. Actual chemostat dilution rates in Table S2.

carbon_growth.citation: Nomura et al. Electron flow in hydrogenotrophic methanogens under nickel limitation. Nature (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41586-025-09229-y. [PMC12350162]
